Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Embracing Bayesian methods in clinical trials 60 upvotes, 4 comments
  2. Can It Resolve DOOM? Game Engine in 2k DNS Records 32 upvotes, 2 comments
  3. Browser-based SFX synthesizer using WASM/Zig 24 upvotes, 2 comments
  4. Explore the Hidden World of Sand 146 upvotes, 32 comments
  5. Meow.camera 139 upvotes, 32 comments
  6. Nashville library launches Memory Lab for digitizing home movies 61 upvotes, 12 comments
  7. Installing a Let's Encrypt TLS certificate on a Brother printer with Certbot 165 upvotes, 45 comments
  8. Building FireStriker: Making Civic Tech Free 68 upvotes, 15 comments
  9. ISBN Visualization – Annas Archive 32 upvotes, 6 comments
  10. LG's new 1Hz display is the secret behind a new laptop's battery life 35 upvotes, 8 comments
  11. Desk for people who work at home with a cat 293 upvotes, 116 comments
  12. Velxio 2.0 – Emulate Arduino, ESP32, and Raspberry Pi 3 in the Browser 31 upvotes, 7 comments
  13. If you don't opt out by Apr 24 GitHub will train on your private repos 280 upvotes, 120 comments
  14. Gzip decompression in 250 lines of Rust 98 upvotes, 36 comments
  15. Capability-Based Security for Redox: Namespace and CWD as Capabilities 7 upvotes, 0 comments
  16. Anatomy of the .claude/ folder 317 upvotes, 160 comments
  17. A Faster Alternative to Jq 354 upvotes, 225 comments
  18. Ask HN: Founders of estonian e-businesses – is it worth it? 67 upvotes, 37 comments
  19. Make macOS consistently bad (unironically) 181 upvotes, 122 comments
  20. EMachines never obsolete PCs: More than a meme 56 upvotes, 33 comments
  21. Hold on to Your Hardware 531 upvotes, 435 comments
  22. People inside Microsoft are fighting to drop mandatory Microsoft Account 416 upvotes, 346 comments
  23. The Future of SCIP 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  24. Schedule tasks on the web 277 upvotes, 225 comments
  25. ‘Energy independence feels practical’: Europeans building mini solar farms 173 upvotes, 152 comments
  26. Show HN: Open-Source Animal Crossing–Style UI for Claude Code Agents 36 upvotes, 27 comments
  27. Slovenia becomes first EU country to introduce fuel rationing 45 upvotes, 40 comments
  28. Telnyx package compromised on PyPI 58 upvotes, 70 comments
  29. 21,864 Yugoslavian .yu domains 61 upvotes, 83 comments
  30. Should QA exist? 77 upvotes, 113 comments

Last generated 6:00am, 28 March 2026. Source here.
